skill.name: backend-models
skill.description: Define database models and ORM entities with clear naming, relationships, validation rules, constraints, indexes, timestamps, and model methods.
skill.summary: Guide backend model and ORM entity design for stronger data integrity.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported path traversal and weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. The weak cryptography findings at lines 3 and 23 are false positives from prose, but line 28 uses an upward relative link to an external standards file outside the packaged skill. No evidence found of malicious intent, command execution, network access, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Upward Relative Documentation Link
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 28 points to ../../../agent-os/standards/backend/models.md. This appears to be a documentation dependency, but it leaves the skill package and could cause an agent to read unbundled local content if that path exists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The upward relative path is directly present and crosses package boundaries. Confidence is medium because the line is a Markdown reference, not executable file access or confirmed data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3 and 23. These lines describe backend models and coding standards in prose; no cryptographic algorithm use or implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language skill descriptions only. There is no code, no hash function call, and no cryptographic configuration to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Backend data models often drift into inconsistent names, weak constraints, and unclear relationships. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward structured ORM models with better data integrity.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of model classes and database table definitions.
Covers one-to-many, many-to-many, and belongs-to relationship design.
Prompts validation rules, database constraints, indexes, and data types.
Addresses timestamps, foreign keys, cascade behavior, and query helpers.
Applies to ORM contexts including ActiveRecord, SQLAlchemy, Sequelize, and Prisma.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not execute migrations.
The packaged skill contains only SKILL.md and references an external standards file.
Framework-specific output depends on project files and conventions supplied by the user.
It does not inspect a live database for existing constraints or indexes.

use_cases.title: Create a New Domain Model
use_cases.description: Define fields, relationships, indexes, validations, and timestamps before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review ORM Entity Changes
use_cases.description: Check a proposed model update for missing constraints, unclear relationship names, or risky cascade behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize Data Modeling Practices
use_cases.description: Apply consistent naming, validation, and integrity rules across model files in a project.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-models skill to design a model for [entity]. Include fields, required validations, timestamps, and basic indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Add Relationships
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-models skill to add relationships between [model A] and [model B]. Include cardinality, foreign keys, and cascade behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review Model Integrity
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-models skill to review these ORM models for missing constraints, weak validations, naming issues, and index gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Model Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-models skill to plan a refactor of this model layer. Preserve existing behavior while improving relationships, constraints, indexes, and query helpers.

output_examples.input: Design a model for customer subscriptions.
output_examples.output: A structured model plan with subscription fields, account relationship, status validation, billing indexes, timestamps, and safe cascade guidance.

output_examples.input: Review these ORM entities before migration.
output_examples.output: A review summary identifying missing unique constraints, unclear relationship ownership, weak nullability choices, and recommended index additions.

output_examples.input: Refactor order and order item models.
output_examples.output: A migration-aware refactor plan that separates responsibilities, clarifies foreign keys, preserves historical data, and documents validation changes.

best_practices: Provide the target ORM, framework, and existing model files before asking for changes.
Ask for validations and database constraints together so application and database rules stay aligned.
Review relationship ownership, cascade behavior, and indexes before generating migrations.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for model code without sharing the framework or existing naming conventions.
Do not rely on application validations when database constraints are required for integrity.
Do not add cascade deletes without confirming retention and audit requirements.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design and review backend models, ORM entities, relationships, validations, constraints, indexes, and model helper methods.

faq.question: Which ORMs does it mention?
faq.answer: The skill explicitly mentions ActiveRecord, SQLAlchemy, Sequelize, Prisma schemas, and general ORM model definitions.

faq.question: Can it create database migrations?
faq.answer: It can guide migration-ready model design, but it does not execute migrations or inspect a live database.

faq.question: Why is there a filesystem warning?
faq.answer: SKILL.md references ../../../agent-os/standards/backend/models.md, which points outside the packaged skill directory.

faq.question: Is the weak cryptography alert confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The cited lines contain prose about backend models and coding standards, not cryptographic code or configuration.

faq.question: What context should users provide?
faq.answer: Users should provide the framework, ORM, existing model files, relationship requirements, and any data retention rules.
